description: High-level governance and authorization document defining project purpose, objectives, scope, key stakeholders, decision rights, milestones, and approval requirements tailored to establishing a 4-bed Bonn residential parasomnia unit.
document_template_secondary: Internal University Template for Governance Documents
steps_to_create: Articulate project purpose and success criteria
Define high-level scope and boundaries
Identify key stakeholders and governance structure
Draft roles, responsibilities, and decision rights
Outline major milestones and high-level schedule
Specify approvals and escalation paths
approval_authorities: PI, Steering Committee, Legal/Regulatory Affairs Lead

description: Plan to identify, classify, and engage stakeholders (local residents, University Hospital Bonn, regulatory bodies, funders, potential advisory boards) with defined communication cadences and feedback mechanisms.
steps_to_create: List primary/secondary stakeholders
Assess interests/impact and influence
Define engagement objectives and channels
Develop engagement activities (meetings, briefings, advisory board)
Establish communications schedule and success metrics
Obtain approvals and assign owners
approval_authorities: PI, Steering Committee, Community Advisory Board Lead

description: Integrated risk register capturing identified risks across recruitment, safety, privacy, finances, and operations with owners, probabilistic scoring, mitigation actions, triggers, and contingency planning.
steps_to_create: Catalog project risks from all domains (safety, ethics, governance, budget, schedule)
Assess likelihood and impact using standard scales
Assign risk owners and mitigation actions
Define risk triggers and monitoring cadence
Link risks to contingency plans and budget buffers
Review and sign-off by governance bodies

description: Top-level budget allocating funds by aim (Aim 1, Aim 2, Aim 3) with contingencies, funding sources, and governance for financial decision-making.
steps_to_create: Define cost categories (facilities, equipment, personnel, operations)
Allocate funds by aims (50/30/20) with rationale
Identify contingency reserves and risk-adjusted buffers
Map potential funding sources (grants, internal funds)
Create governance processes for budget changes and approvals
Establish monthly/quarterly budget monitoring
approval_authorities: PI, Finance Director, Grant Administrator, Steering Committee

description: Strategic plan outlining recruitment channels (hospital sleep clinic referrals, regional neurologists, online campaigns, patient groups), screening burden, representativeness, cost implications, and alignment with data acquisition needs.
steps_to_create: Define recruitment channels and target populations
Estimate expected yields and screening burden per channel
Assess potential biases and representativeness implications
Estimate resource needs and costs per channel
Coordinate with Data Acquisition Strategy for compatibility
Secure approvals from ethics and governance bodies

description: Policy-level framework to decide data collection intensity (low-burden sensors vs PSG scheduling), criteria for escalation, safety considerations, and data quality targets.
responsible_role_type: Computational Neuroscience Postdoctoral Researcher / Sleep Technologist
steps_to_create: Define data streams (headbands, mattress sensors, PSG) and corresponding burden
Establish escalation rules for PSG nights based on data signals
Set data quality metrics (signal-to-noise, completion rates)
Incorporate safety and participant comfort requirements
Plan scheduling logistics and staffing implications
Obtain required approvals and align with privacy controls
